summary: David’s sinful census brings plague on Israel, which ends when he builds an altar at Araunah’s threshing floor — the site that will become the temple — and David charges Solomon with the massive task of building it, while Psalm 30 exults in God’s reversal of mourning into dancing.
psalm_rows_html:
TypeAuthorSituationKey ThemeNotable VerseNT Connection
Individual ThanksgivingDavidDedication of the temple (or house); deliverance from illness or near-death experienceGod turns mourning into dancing; he restores the soul from Sheol and clothes the penitent with gladness"Weeping may tarry for the night, but joy comes with the morning." (v. 5)The pattern of death and resurrection — Christ's passion and Easter morning perfectly fulfill the movement from mourning to dancing (John 16:20)
